# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Python CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout

    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python
      with:
        python-version: '3.x' # Automatically get the latest Python 3 version

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt # Assumes you have a requirements.txt

    - name: Run tests
      run: |
        # Replace with your actual test command
        # e.g., pytest, python -m unittest discover
        echo "Running application tests..."
        # pytest # Uncomment if you use pytest
        # python -m unittest discover # Uncomment if you use unittest

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-test
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout

    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python
      with:
        python-version: '3.x'

    - name: Deploy application
      run: |
        echo "Deploying application to production..."
        # Add your deployment commands here.
        # This might involve:
        # - Building a Docker image and pushing to a registry
        # - Running a deployment script (e.g., python deploy.py)
        # - Using a cloud provider CLI (e.g., aws s3 sync, gcloud app deploy)
        # For example:
        # pip install boto3
        # python deploy_to_s3.py
        # gcloud app deploy app.yaml --version=v$(date +'%Y%m%d%H%M%S') --no-promote
        echo "Deployment complete!"
